rationale: This package documents a classic sdclt.exe UAC bypass chain. EV-001 and EV-002 show PowerShell writing a malicious command and an empty DelegateExecute value to HKU\...\Folder\shell\open\command — the well-known registry hijack that causes sdclt.exe to auto-elevate and execute the attacker-controlled payload. A0 confirms sdclt.exe was then launched (integrity_level: High) from cmd.exe, and EV-003 shows it spawned control.exe with /name Microsoft.BackupAndRestoreCenter, which in turn (EV-004) spawned PowerShell at High integrity running a steganographic payload loader that reads pixel data from monkey.png and IEX-executes the decoded content — a confirmed in-memory code execution technique. EV-005 shows the registry key was subsequently deleted, indicating deliberate cleanup. The full UAC bypass → elevated PowerShell → steganography-based payload execution chain is confirmed across all evidence items; no benign explanation accounts for this sequence.
